pages.text: $(italic)Curios$() gives you a second set of equipment slots — for $(bold)accessories$(): the things you wear that aren't armour. Rings, belts, charms, pendants, a pack on your back. A handful of mods in Novus quietly hang their gear here instead of cluttering your hotbar, so it's worth knowing the drawer exists.
pages.title: Accessory Slots

pages.text: $(bold)Open it.$() There's a small $(italic)button at the top-left corner$() of your inventory screen — click it to fold out the accessory slots beside your character. (You can also bind a key: $(italic)Open Curios$(), unbound by default.)$(br2)Each accessory knows its own slot — a ring won't go on your belt. Slots reveal themselves as you collect things that use them, so an empty start fills out as you gather gear.
pages.title: Open It

pages.text: $(bold)What goes where, in Novus:$()$(br)$(li)$(bold)Belt$() — the Supplementaries $(italic)Quiver$().$(li)$(bold)Back$() — your $(italic)Backpack$().$(li)$(bold)Ring$(), $(bold)Head$(), $(bold)Necklace$() — $(italic)Botania$()'s rings, its flight tiara, and pendants.$(li)$(bold)Charm$() / $(bold)Curio$() — catch-all slots for trinkets and badges from across the pack.$(br2)Worn accessories work $(italic)passively$(): no button to press, they do their job as long as they're slotted.
pages.title: What Goes Where
